Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. The Collective Ambition Behind Odysseus, a Game-Changing Sci-Fi Larp 75 upvotes, 3 comments
  2. CasNum 318 upvotes, 38 comments
  3. Phi-4-reasoning-vision and the lessons of training a multimodal reasoning model 63 upvotes, 4 comments
  4. What if the Apple ][ had run on Field-Sequential? 37 upvotes, 2 comments
  5. Some Words on WigglyPaint 83 upvotes, 9 comments
  6. LibreOffice: Request to the European Commission to adhere to its own guidances 135 upvotes, 24 comments
  7. If It Quacks Like a Package Manager 27 upvotes, 2 comments
  8. Sem – Semantic version control. Entity-level diffs on top of Git 66 upvotes, 10 comments
  9. FrameBook 134 upvotes, 26 comments
  10. Rijksmuseum researchers discover new painting by Rembrandt van Rijn 103 upvotes, 23 comments
  11. How to run Qwen 3.5 locally 363 upvotes, 111 comments
  12. Show HN: Curiosity – DIY 6" Newtonian Reflector Telescope 68 upvotes, 16 comments
  13. Show HN: Eyot, A programming language where the GPU is just another thread 48 upvotes, 10 comments
  14. I ported Linux to the PS5 and turned it into a Steam Machine 213 upvotes, 78 comments
  15. Claude Struggles to Cope with ChatGPT Exodus 8 upvotes, 0 comments
  16. Based on its own charter, OpenAI should surrender the race 35 upvotes, 8 comments
  17. SWE-CI: Evaluating Agent Capabilities in Maintaining Codebases via CI 95 upvotes, 34 comments
  18. Notes on Writing WASM 136 upvotes, 53 comments
  19. Cloud VM benchmarks 2026 300 upvotes, 134 comments
  20. CLI RSS/Atom feed reader inspired by Taskwarrior, synced using Git 38 upvotes, 12 comments
  21. The Lobster Programming Language 92 upvotes, 38 comments
  22. Beagle, a source code management system that stores AST trees 42 upvotes, 15 comments
  23. LibreOffice Writer now supports Markdown 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  24. Apple's 512GB Mac Studio vanishes, a quiet acknowledgment of the RAM shortage 282 upvotes, 151 comments
  25. Warn about PyPy being unmaintained 271 upvotes, 145 comments
  26. Log messages are mostly for the people operating your software 18 upvotes, 5 comments
  27. Accessing inactive union members through char 9 upvotes, 2 comments
  28. Why can't you tune your guitar? (2019) 82 upvotes, 67 comments
  29. Oracle may slash up to 30k jobs to fund AI data-centers as US banks retreat 107 upvotes, 96 comments
  30. My Homelab Setup 4 upvotes, 2 comments

Last generated 1:30am, 9 March 2026. Source here.
